phenomenon: automatic processing of stimulus-response contingencies
constructs: attention modulation contingency prediction response threshold
claim: Differential Stroop effects arise from learned contingencies between stimuli and responses, not from flexible modulation of attention to stimulus properties.

phenomenon: information search strategy and inference bias
constructs: sampling strategy inductive inference feedback bias
claim: All feasible information-gathering strategies introduce systematic biases for some inferences even when samples remain unbiased, creating an intractable dilemma in probabilistic reasoning.

phenomenon: rejection of remembered items in short-term memory
constructs: familiarity recollection context retrieval item memory
claim: Recognition decisions rely on dual processes where recollection can access memory via either context-to-item or item-to-context routes, with intrusions showing parallel processing patterns.

phenomenon: context-dependent forgetting of learned material
constructs: context strength item strength spaced repetition directed forgetting
claim: Intentional forgetting is enhanced for strong items only when strength derives from context reinstatement, not from item-level processing, supporting a context-based forgetting mechanism.

phenomenon: language experience and executive control differences
constructs: bilingualism inhibition action goal maintenance reactive inhibition
claim: Bilinguals show enhanced ability to maintain action goals rather than superior active inhibition, leading indirectly to stronger reactive suppression of interference.

phenomenon: semantic structure influence on automatic arithmetic activation
constructs: analogical priming semantic relations arithmetic facts activation
claim: Automatic activation of arithmetic facts depends on whether the semantic structure of priming context aligns with the relational structure of the arithmetic operation.

phenomenon: implicit learning of sequential patterns and errors
constructs: implicit learning repetition error learning response probability
claim: Implicit learning of repeated sequences extends to learning error patterns, with response probability increasing for both correct and incorrect prior responses.

phenomenon: processing stages in lexical decision making
constructs: stimulus quality word frequency cascaded processing discrete processing
claim: Additive effects of stimulus quality and word frequency mask opposing interactive processes in response-time distributions that reveal different processing architectures depending on available discriminability cues.

phenomenon: semantic interference in language production timing
constructs: semantic interference lexical selection postlexical processing picture naming
claim: Semantic interference effects emerge from postlexical processing stages rather than lexical selection bottlenecks, occurring even when response selection is delayed.

phenomenon: dimensionality of visual statistical learning
constructs: statistical learning feature binding object recognition feature covariance
claim: Visual statistical learning operates flexibly as object-based or feature-based depending on how feature dimensions covary, with feature sensitivity defining what counts as a perceptual unit.

phenomenon: causal inference from joint occurrence patterns
constructs: contingency causal judgment accounting for occurrences cause strength
claim: Causal judgments center on which cause better accounts for outcome occurrences rather than contingency alone, with presence likelihood and conditional outcome rates jointly determining perceived causal strength.

phenomenon: timing information in sequential memory and order recall
constructs: timing memory temporal structure serial recall positional representation
claim: Temporal information plays multiple roles in short-term memory beyond generating positional codes, with individual duration information directly influencing order memory and recall organization.

phenomenon: stimulus dependencies in learning and attention
constructs: attention associative learning adjacent dependency nonadjacent dependency
claim: Learning both adjacent and nonadjacent dependencies depends on active attentional processing rather than spatial contiguity, making joint attention necessary and sufficient for association formation.

phenomenon: attention protection of working memory contents
constructs: visual working memory attention retroactive cueing interference resistance
claim: Focused attention to a working memory item after encoding makes it resistant to interference from new information, suggesting working memory contents are volatile without sustained attentional focus.

phenomenon: decision-making and learning in uncertain environments
constructs: Bayesian learning risk-taking sequential decision stochastic environment
claim: Sequential risk-taking involves distinct learning and decision processes that jointly adapt to environmental structure, with learning processes potentially undermining assessment diagnosticity.

phenomenon: social rejection of principled moral noncompliance
constructs: moral rebellion implicit reproach self-image threat group pressure
claim: People who break ranks on moral grounds are resented by participants when their principled refusal implicitly threatens the self-image of those who complied, but not by outside observers.

phenomenon: convergence and divergence of attitude measurement approaches
constructs: implicit attitude explicit attitude structural similarity measurement format
claim: Weak correlations between implicit and explicit attitude measures reflect task format differences rather than independent cognitive systems; structural similarity increases correlation substantially.

phenomenon: personality trait reporting development across adolescence
constructs: personality development Big Five coherence differentiation acquiescence
claim: Youth personality self-reports show differential developmental trajectories, with coherence and differentiation increasing heterogeneously across Big Five domains independent of comprehension.

phenomenon: parental attachment as anxiety buffer
constructs: attachment mortality salience death anxiety worldview parental relationships
claim: Parental attachment buffers existential anxiety through reduced death-thought accessibility and enhanced positive memory, with attachment style determining whether adults rely on parents versus romantic partners.

phenomenon: goal-based modulation of behavioral output
constructs: action goals inaction goals cognitive output motor output prime-induced activation
claim: General action and inaction goals modulate diverse forms of behavioral output uniformly, with multiple classes of behavior responding similarly to the same goal-activating stimuli.
